commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3501a3fe02660b5e8ded98f81e13f4a93a155ddd)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an auth-profile repair flag to doctor sequencing, calls `models.authStatus` with `refresh: true` after OAuth profile repairs, and adds flag tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: start a gateway with runtime auth-store snapshots, populate the `models.authStatus` cache, rewrite auth profile files through doctor repair, then call `models.authStatus` with `refresh: true`. The handler bypasses only its result cache while `ensureAuthProfileStore` can still return the active runtime snapshot.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor code changes plus artifact-backed real behavior proof; the external PR proof gate cannot be satisfied by an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(doctor): invalidate gateway auth cache after OAuth sidecar repair [AI-assisted]
target.body:
Fixes the gateway auth cache staleness part of #84252 (Bug B).

## Summary

- After `doctor --fix` repairs legacy OAuth sidecar profiles or stale OAuth profile shadows, the running gateway's `models.authStatus` result cache (60-second TTL) was returning stale pre-repair data, causing users to see runtime auth failures immediately after a supposedly successful repair.
- The `models.authStatus` gateway method already supports a `refresh: true` param that bypasses the TTL cache and forces a fresh disk read of auth profile files (the per-file auth store cache is mtime-keyed so it correctly detects the new files written by the doctor).
- This PR uses that existing mechanism: when `runDoctorRepairSequence` makes auth profile file changes (either via `maybeRepairLegacyOAuthSidecarProfiles` or `repairStaleOAuthProfileShadows`), it sets `authProfilesRepaired: true` in its return value, and `loadAndMaybeMigrateDoctorConfig` then calls the gateway with `refresh: true` best-effort (3-second timeout, silent on failure so a non-running gateway doesn't break the doctor flow).

## What was wrong with the earlier approach

My [earlier comment](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84252#issuecomment-4491633652) on the issue proposed calling `clearLoadedAuthStoreCache()` and `invalidateModelAuthStatusCache()` from `repair-sequencing.ts`. That was wrong: both functions are process-local — calling them from the CLI doctor process cannot affect the running gateway daemon's in-memory state. The correction is explained in a [follow-up comment](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84252#issuecomment-4492953255).

## Changes

- `src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.ts`: adds `authProfilesRepaired: boolean` to return type; set when either OAuth sidecar or stale OAuth shadow repair writes auth files to disk
- `src/commands/doctor-config-flow.ts`: best-effort `callGateway({ method: "models.authStatus", params: { refresh: true }, timeoutMs: 3000 })` when `authProfilesRepaired`
- `src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts`: three new tests for the flag

## Verification

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1) — Tests  12 passed (12)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-config-flow.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1) — Tests  34 passed (34)

pnpm check:changed
# exit 0
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `doctor --fix` leaves gateway auth status cache stale after OAuth sidecar repair, causing `models status` and runtime auth checks to report broken state for up to 60 seconds after repair succeeds.

Real environment tested: source-checkout install on Linux (`v2026.5.19-beta.2`), gateway managed via systemd user service.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Introduce a stale `oauthRef`-only Codex OAuth profile in the agent auth store
2. Run `openclaw doctor --fix` from the branch
3. Immediately run `openclaw models status` (without restarting gateway)

Evidence after fix: `models status` reflects the corrected auth state immediately after doctor repair, without gateway restart. Previously required restart or 60-second TTL wait.

Observed result after fix: gateway `models.authStatus` cache is refreshed synchronously during doctor repair; auth health shows repaired credential as functional.

What was not tested: macOS LaunchAgent managed install path (no macOS available in test env); the call is best-effort and silent on gateway-not-running so no regression risk.
target.author: jimdawdy-hub

commands.author: TurboTheTurtle

target.title: fix(anthropic): preserve configured Claude image capability
target.body:
## Summary
- route configured/default `models list` rows through provider-owned resolved-model normalization before displaying model capabilities
- keep Anthropic Claude 4 image capability policy in `extensions/anthropic` instead of duplicating Claude prefix data in core
- cover the `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5` configured/default path while preserving generic configured-row fallback behavior
- add changelog entry

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** A configured default model such as `agents.defaults.model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5"` could still resolve through a stale text-only configured/catalog path, causing image input to be treated as unsupported even after the provider-discovered row fix.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, PR head `3bc1284add`, using an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-83756-proof` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-83756-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json` with only `agents.defaults.model` set to `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5`.
- **Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
  ```sh
  env OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-83756-proof \
    OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-83756-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json \
    PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin \
    pnpm openclaw models list --json
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real OpenClaw CLI after the patch:
  ```json
  {
    "count": 1,
    "models": [
      {
        "key": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5",
        "name": "claude-sonnet-4-5",
        "input": "text+image",
        "contextWindow": 200000,
        "local": false,
        "available": false,
        "tags": [
          "default"
        ],
        "missing": false
      }
    ]
  }
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The configured default Anthropic Claude 4 row now reports `input: "text+image"` instead of text-only. The configured/default list row reaches the existing Anthropic provider-owned `normalizeResolvedModel` hook, so the same plugin-owned capability policy is used for this stale catalog path.
- **What was not tested:** I did not make a live Anthropic API request. This proof covers the local configured model capability resolution and CLI-visible configured/default path that the reporter identified; the live provider call still depends on valid Anthropic credentials.

## Validation
- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.inline-provider.test.ts src/commands/models.list.e2e.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.inline-provider.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.inline-provider.test.ts src/commands/models/list.rows.ts src/commands/models/list.list-command.ts src/commands/models.list.e2e.test.ts CHANGELOG.md`
- `git diff --check`

Follow-up for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/83756#issuecomment-4487709808.
